Tooltip_PCP_CrushedCharcoal:
Finely ground charcoal powder, ready for purification.
Produced by crushing wood charcoal in a mortar and pestle.
Tooltip_PCP_PurifiedCharcoal:
High-purity carbon powder with mineral impurities removed.
Essential reagent for chemical synthesis.
Tooltip_PCP_Potash:
Potassium carbonate (K2CO3) extracted from charcoal during
purification. Combined with quicklime to synthesize
potassium hydroxide (KOH) for biodiesel production.
Tooltip_PCP_DilutedCompost:
Compost dissolved in water, forming a nitrate-rich slurry.
Can be processed into potassium nitrate.
Tooltip_PCP_SulphurPowder:
A fine yellow granular powder of elemental sulphur.
Used in the production of blackpowder.
Tooltip_PCP_PotassiumNitratePowder:
White crystalline granular powder of potassium nitrate (saltpeter).
The primary oxidiser in blackpowder.
Tooltip_PCP_SulphuricAcidVessel:
Concentrated sulphuric acid (H2SO4) in an acid-resistant vessel.
WARNING: EXTREMELY CORROSIVE. Ingestion is fatal.
Handle only with protective gloves and eyewear.
Tooltip_PCP_LeadScrap:
Salvaged lead plates from a dismantled car battery.
Heavy but valuable for constructing deep-cycle batteries
or melting down for other uses.
Tooltip_PCP_PlasticScrap:
Small pieces of cut plastic salvaged from bottles and containers.
Can be shaped into battery casings or used as insulation.
Tooltip_PCP_AcidWashedElectronics:
Electronic components cleaned with dilute sulphuric acid.
Oxide layers and corrosion removed, exposing pristine
copper traces. Superior quality for precision assembly.
Tooltip_PCP_BkChemistryPathways:
The complete compendium of all Phobos' Industrial Pathways: Biomass recipes.
Covers every crafting category: field chemistry, kitchen chemistry,
laboratory chemistry, industrial processes, and horticulture.

An extremely rare find. Most survivors will only ever find
the individual category primers.

Reading this book unlocks ALL chemistry recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_BkFieldChemistry:
A practical field guide to chemistry without laboratory equipment.
Covers charcoal processing, sulphur extraction, blackpowder
synthesis, and basic purification techniques.

Reading this book unlocks all Field Chemistry recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_BkKitchenChemistry:
A companion guide to kitchen-based chemical processes.
Covers fat rendering, soap-making, bone char pyrolysis,
salt extraction, and cooking-pot reactions.

Reading this book unlocks all Kitchen Chemistry recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_BkLabChemistry:
A comprehensive laboratory chemistry reference manual.
Covers acid synthesis, advanced distillation, centrifuge
separation, chromatography, and precision reactions.

Reading this book unlocks all Lab Chemistry recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_BkIndustrialChemistry:
A technical manual for industrial-scale chemical processing.
Covers concrete mixing, bulk synthesis, and powered
workstation operations.

Reading this book unlocks all Industrial Chemistry recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_BkHorticulture:
A botanical guide to plant processing and horticulture.
Covers hemp processing, tobacco curing, papermaking,
smoking products, and agricultural applications.

Reading this book unlocks all Horticulture recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_CrudeVegetableOil:
Crude pressed oil from seeds or beans, cloudy with plant matter.
Suitable for transesterification into biodiesel.
Tooltip_PCP_RenderedFat:
Animal fat rendered down to a clean, usable form.
Can substitute for vegetable oil in biodiesel production.
Tooltip_PCP_WoodMethanol:
Methanol (wood alcohol) obtained by destructive distillation.
WARNING: EXTREMELY TOXIC. Ingestion causes blindness and death.
Essential reagent for biodiesel transesterification.
Tooltip_PCP_WoodTar:
Thick, dark tar condensed during wood distillation.
Useful as a waterproofing sealant or fuel supplement.
Tooltip_PCP_PotassiumHydroxide:
Potassium hydroxide (KOH), a powerful caustic alkali.
WARNING: Causes severe chemical burns on contact.
Catalyst for transesterification of fats into biodiesel.
Tooltip_PCP_Calcite:
Calcium carbonate (chalk/calcite) precipitated during
KOH synthesis. Useful as a soil amendment or abrasive.
Tooltip_PCP_CrudeBiodiesel:
Raw biodiesel (fatty acid methyl esters) freshly produced
by transesterification. Contains dissolved glycerol and
soap residue. Must be washed before use as fuel.
Tooltip_PCP_Glycerol:
Glycerol (glycerine) separated during transesterification.
A versatile by-product for soap-making and medicine.
Tooltip_PCP_WashedBiodiesel:
Water-washed biodiesel with soap and glycerol removed.
After settling, this is ready to be decanted as usable fuel.
Tooltip_PCP_RefinedBiodieselCan:
Home-refined biodiesel, suitable for use in engines.
Produced through transesterification and water-washing
of vegetable oil or animal fat.
Tooltip_PCP_BoneChar:
Porous carbon produced by pyrolysing animal bones in a sealed vessel.
Retains calcium phosphate matrix, giving it superior adsorption
properties compared to wood charcoal. Historically used for
sugar refining and water purification.
Tooltip_PCP_CrudeSoap:
Crude lye soap made from glycerol and caustic alkali.
Not pretty, but effective for cleaning and hygiene.
Tooltip_PCP_BoneMeal:
Ground bone char powder.
Rich in calcium and phosphorus.
Used in animal feed supplements and fertiliser.
Tooltip_PCP_MineralFeedSupplement:
Mineral-enriched animal feed.
Provides essential calcium, phosphorus, and potassium.
Use to feed livestock.
Tooltip_PCP_ActivatedCarbon:
Chemically activated carbon with high porosity.
Excellent adsorbent for water purification
and gas mask filtration.
Tooltip_PCP_SulphurFungicideSpray:
Use on crops to treat mildew. Right-click a
growing plant and select Treat Problem to apply.
Made from sulphur powder dissolved in water.
Tooltip_PCP_InsecticidalSoapSpray:
Use on crops to treat aphid infestations.
Right-click a growing plant and select Treat
Problem to apply. Made from soap and water.
Tooltip_PCP_PotashFoliarSpray:
Use on crops to treat pest fly infestations.
Right-click a growing plant and select Treat
Problem to apply. Made from potash and water.
Tooltip_PCP_BrineJar:
Mineral-rich brine water collected from a well.
Contains dissolved sodium chloride and trace minerals.
Must be concentrated and crystallized to extract salt.
Tooltip_PCP_BrineConcentrate:
Thick brine concentrate produced by evaporating water.
Ready for crystallization into crude salt.
Tooltip_PCP_CoarseSalt:
Unrefined salt crystals with mineral impurities.
Filter through cheesecloth and recrystallize for table salt.
Tooltip_PCP_CollectBrineAction:
Collect mineral-rich brine water from this well.
Requires an empty container (jar, bottle, or bucket).
Tooltip_PCP_NeedEmptyContainer: Requires: Empty container (jar, bottle, bucket, clay jar, or crucible)
Tooltip_PCP_MortarMix:
Calcite-based mortar binding agent.
Strong adhesive for masonry and brickwork construction.
Tooltip_PCP_StuccoMix:
Plaster-based stucco finishing material.
Used for rendering walls and exterior surfaces.
Tooltip_PCP_ReinforcedConcrete:
Concrete reinforced with scrap metal for structural strength.
Superior building material for fortifications and foundations.
Tooltip_PCP_Fireclay:
Refractory clay blend resistant to extreme heat.
Used in forge and kiln construction or upgrades.
Tooltip_PCP_RettedHempStalk:
Hemp stalk soaked in alkaline solution to loosen bast fibers.
Chemical retting produces higher-quality fiber than water retting.
Tooltip_PCP_HempBastFiber:
Long, strong fibers extracted from retted hemp stalks.
The primary material for spinning twine, weaving cloth,
and producing paper pulp.
Tooltip_PCP_HempHurd:
Woody core fragments separated from hemp bast fiber.
Lightweight and absorbent. Used for charcoal production,
hempcrete blocks, composting, and fire bundles.
Tooltip_PCP_HempTwine:
Simple cordage hand-spun from hemp bast fibers.
Strong and durable. Used for braiding rope
and binding fire bundles.
Tooltip_PCP_HempRope:
Heavy-duty braided rope made from hemp twine.
Resistant to stretching and abrasion.
Can be tarred for waterproofing.
Tooltip_PCP_HempCloth:
Durable woven fabric produced from hemp bast fibers.
Coarser than cotton but far stronger.
Suitable for bandages, canvas, and clothing patches.
Tooltip_PCP_HempCanvas:
Thick, heavy-duty hemp fabric layered for strength.
Historically used for sails, tarpaulins, and tents.
The word 'canvas' derives from 'cannabis'.
Tooltip_PCP_HempPulp:
Softened hemp fiber broken down into crude paper pulp.
Ready for pressing into paper sheets.
Lab-grade NaOH pulping produces higher quality.
Tooltip_PCP_HempPaper:
Paper sheets pressed from hemp pulp.
More durable than wood-pulp paper and resistant to yellowing.
Pre-1883, most paper was made from hemp.
Tooltip_PCP_HempPoultice:
A warm herbal compress of hemp fiber infused with medicinal herbs.
Right-click to apply: reduces pain on all body parts
and relieves stress. Consumed on use.
Tooltip_PCP_HempTincture:
Concentrated herbal extract produced by alcohol extraction
of bioactive compounds from hemp fiber.
Right-click to take a dose: strong pain and stress relief,
reduces unhappiness and boredom, causes mild drowsiness.
5 doses per bottle.
Tooltip_PCP_HempcreteBlock:
Lightweight building block made from hemp hurds and lime.
Excellent thermal insulation and moisture regulation.
Carbon-negative construction material.
Tooltip_PCP_TarredHempRope:
Hemp rope coated in hot wood tar for waterproofing.
Resists rot, mildew, and abrasion in wet conditions.
Traditionally used for rigging, well buckets, and outdoor lashing.
Tooltip_PCP_Oakum:
Loose hemp fibre soaked in wood tar.
Used historically for caulking and waterproofing
— also makes effective tinder.
Tooltip_PCP_SeedPressCake:
Compressed seed residue from oil pressing.
Rich in nitrogen — useful as compost or fertiliser.
Tooltip_PCP_HempSack:
A sturdy woven sack stitched from hemp canvas.
Worn on the back for extra carrying capacity.
Tooltip_PCP_HempFishingNet:
A hand-knotted fishing net woven from hemp twine.
Functions identically to a standard net.
Tooltip_PCP_HempSheetRope:
A hemp rope reinforced with cloth strips.
Can be attached to windows for escape.
Tooltip_PCP_HempSnare:
A simple loop snare fashioned from hemp twine
and a stick. Set near animal paths to catch small game.
Tooltip_PCP_TobaccoWet:
Freshly harvested tobacco leaves, still damp.
Needs to air-dry before use. Leave in inventory or
hang to dry naturally.
Tooltip_PCP_ChewingTobaccoMixRaw:
A moist blend of dried tobacco, salt, sweetener,
and alkali. Place on a heat source to fire-cure
instantly, or seal in a jar for 14-day fermentation.
Tooltip_PCP_SealedChewingTobaccoJar:
Chewing tobacco mix sealed in a glass jar.
Leave sealed for approximately 2 weeks — the freshness
bar shows curing progress, not spoilage.
Tooltip_PCP_CuredChewingTobaccoJar:
Fully cured chewing tobacco in a sealed jar.
Open the jar to retrieve the finished tobacco.
Tooltip_PCP_ChewingTobacco:
Authentically prepared chewing tobacco, fire-cured
or jar-fermented. Relieves stress but causes nausea.
Use sparingly.
Tooltip_PCP_ChewingTobaccoTin:
Chewing tobacco packed in a tin can.
Relieves stress but causes nausea. Use sparingly.
Tooltip_PCP_ChewingTobaccoWaterTin:
Chewing tobacco packed in a water ration tin.
Relieves stress but causes nausea. Use sparingly.
Tooltip_PCP_ChewingTobaccoJar:
Chewing tobacco packed in a glass jar.
Relieves stress but causes nausea. Use sparingly.
Tooltip_PCP_HempBuds:
Fresh hemp flower buds harvested from the plant.
Can be cured by slow drying or decarboxylated by
heating to activate medicinal compounds.
Tooltip_PCP_HempBudsCured:
Slowly dried and cured hemp buds.
More potent than fresh buds. Can be further
decarboxylated by cooking.
Tooltip_PCP_HempBudsDecarbed:
Hemp buds heated to activate cannabinoid compounds.
Edible: reduces stress, boredom, and pain but tastes
terrible. Also used for extraction and smoking.
Tooltip_PCP_CannedHempBuds:
Fresh hemp buds sealed in a container for curing.
Leave sealed for approximately 4 weeks — the freshness
bar shows curing progress, not spoilage.
Can also be cooked to decarboxylate.
Tooltip_PCP_CannedHempBudsCured:
Cured hemp buds in a sealed container.
Fermentation complete. Cook to decarboxylate
or open to use the cured buds.
Tooltip_PCP_CannedHempBudsDecarbed:
Decarboxylated hemp buds in a sealed container.
Ready to be opened and used.
Tooltip_PCP_CannedHempBudsOpen:
Opened container of cured hemp buds.
Use portions for smoking, cooking, or extraction.
Tooltip_PCP_CannedHempBudsDecarbedOpen:
Opened container of decarboxylated hemp buds.
Ready to use in recipes or for smoking.
Tooltip_PCP_HempLoose:
Ground hemp in a resealable pouch.
Pack into pipes, roll into cigarettes or cigars,
or infuse into butter or oil for edible preparations.
Tooltip_PCP_PaperPulpPot:
A pot of hemp fiber simmering in water to break down
into paper pulp. Needs to finish cooking on a heat source.
Tooltip_PCP_PaperPulpPotForged:
A forged pot of hemp fiber simmering in water to break
down into paper pulp. Needs to finish cooking on a heat source.
Tooltip_PCP_PaperPulpPotCooked:
Boiled hemp fiber pulp ready to be formed into
a paper sheet using a mould and deckle.
Tooltip_PCP_PaperPulpPotForgedCooked:
Boiled hemp fiber pulp (forged pot) ready to be formed
into a paper sheet using a mould and deckle.
Tooltip_PCP_MouldAndDeckle:
A wooden frame with fine mesh screen used for forming
paper sheets from wet pulp. Reusable papermaking tool.
Tooltip_PCP_MouldAndDecklePaperSheet:
A mould and deckle with a freshly formed wet paper sheet.
The sheet needs to be removed and pressed to dry.
Tooltip_PCP_RollingPapers:
Thin sheets of paper cut to size for rolling cigarettes
or cigars. Made from hemp paper.
Tooltip_PCP_SmokingPipeGlass:
A hand-blown glass smoking pipe.
Reusable. Load with loose hemp or tobacco to smoke.
Tooltip_PCP_SmokingPipeHemp:
A wooden pipe loaded with loose hemp.
Requires a lighter or matches to use.
Relieves stress and reduces pain.
Tooltip_PCP_SmokingPipeGlassHemp:
A glass pipe loaded with loose hemp.
Requires a lighter or matches to use.
Relieves stress and reduces pain.
Tooltip_PCP_SmokingPipeGlassTobacco:
A glass pipe loaded with tobacco.
Requires a lighter or matches to use.
Relieves stress but causes nausea.
Tooltip_PCP_CanPipeHemp:
An improvised pipe made from a tin can, loaded with hemp.
Requires a lighter or matches to use.
Relieves stress and reduces pain.
Tooltip_PCP_CigarHemp:
A thick hand-rolled hemp cigar wrapped in hemp paper.
Strong effects. Relieves significant stress and pain
but causes drowsiness.
Tooltip_PCP_CigarRolled:
A hand-rolled tobacco cigar.
Relieves stress and boredom but causes nausea.
Tooltip_PCP_CigaretteHemp:
A single hemp cigarette rolled in hemp paper.
Mild stress and pain relief. Lighter effects than a cigar.
Tooltip_PCP_CigarettePackHemp:
A pack of hemp cigarettes.
Each use smokes one cigarette for mild stress
and pain relief.
Tooltip_PCP_CigarettePackRolled:
A pack of rolled tobacco cigarettes.
Each use smokes one cigarette for stress relief
but causes nausea.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanHempButter:
Butter melting with decarboxylated hemp in a saucepan.
Leave on a heat source to infuse the cannabinoid compounds.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanHempButterCooked:
Infused hemp butter ready to be strained into a jar.
The fat has absorbed the active compounds.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanHempInfusedOil:
Oil warming with decarboxylated hemp in a saucepan.
Leave on a heat source to extract the active compounds.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanHempInfusedOilCooked:
Infused hemp oil ready to be strained into a jar.
The oil has absorbed the active compounds.
Tooltip_PCP_HempButter:
Butter infused with hemp compounds.
Reduces stress, boredom, and pain but causes drowsiness.
The fat-eating experience tempers the mood boost.
Use as a spice in cooking for evolved recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_HempInfusedOil:
Cooking oil infused with hemp compounds.
Reduces stress, boredom, and pain but causes mild drowsiness.
Lighter effects than hemp butter.
Use as a spice in cooking for evolved recipes.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanSyrup:
A saucepan of sugar water being cooked into syrup.
Leave on a heat source until done.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanCopperSyrup:
A copper saucepan of sugar water being cooked into syrup.
Leave on a heat source until done.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanSyrupCooked:
Cooked sugar syrup ready to be bottled.
Pour into a jar to produce simple sugar syrup.
Tooltip_PCP_SaucepanCopperSyrupCooked:
Cooked sugar syrup (copper saucepan) ready to be bottled.
Pour into a jar to produce simple sugar syrup.
Tooltip_PCP_SimpleSugarSyrup:
Simple sugar syrup made by dissolving sugar in hot water.
A comforting treat that improves mood and relieves boredom.
Use as a sweetener in cooking and beverages.
